spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the feature's general idea but doesn't surface the named spec API symbols directly.

interactive.rationale: Real controls + script-driven side-effects make this hands-on, not a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Most of the blurb's promises land but a few of the named behaviours aren't shown.

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge-case handling — only the success path is demonstrated.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link as required.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex, button overrides keep bg+color paired.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for autofill-event
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: Defines AutofillEvent (extends Event) with autofillValues FrozenArray<AutofillValueEntry> and refill nullable RefillCallback. UA MUST fire autofill event before committing values. refill() rejects InvalidStateError on timeout, when allowRefill is false, or when refill pending. autofillValues entries = [HTMLElement, DOMString]. New autocomplete token: "full-address". The refill attribute becomes nu...

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The page currently shows the happy path. Add a UI affordance that drives the feature into a failure mode the spec explicitly calls out (timeout, denied permission, cross-origin block, malformed input) and surface what the browser actually does.

openQuestions.title: Reconcile blurb and page contents
openQuestions.detail: The feature index blurb promises behaviours that the page doesn't yet exercise. Either trim the blurb to match what the page does, or add the missing UI.

summary: Workable concept with a couple of gaps. 3 open question(s) queued.
